title: 2AM at Paradise Cafe
notes: This album has everything you could want in a jazz record. Mostly comprising of melancholic blues, it varies quite a bit on vibe of each song, which I think makes it quite replayable. Free-flowing transitions between songs make this album sound like it was a single take, further highlighting the musicianship of the songs. Each song is a small vignette of someone you might find at a jazz bar at 2AM.
highlights: The two features (*Big City Blues featuring Mel Torme* and *Blue featuring Sarah Vaughan*) as well as *Say No More*. The harmony between the artists is exceptional. The high energy moments of ballads like *Say No More* and the choice of keys and vibe switch in songs like *Blue* create great replayability.
lowlights: Limited variety in style. The album sometimes sounds like 11 blues songs in a row. Slotting in different styles would break this up.
